signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 159.34; 160.00 remains the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. Fed 3.75% (ON HOLD) vs BoJ 0.75% (ON HOLD) — 450bp carry differential favors USD. Risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — watch 157.00 as the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD LF net long +41,675 (CFTC, week ending Apr 14); AM not reducing. HV30 at 10.3%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 11.2% — options pricing a 0.9 vol-pt premium over realized. 25d RR at −0.22: mild put skew despite the bullish price trend — market hedging rather than chasing. AUD/USD at 0.7157; late-trend caution signal. Watch for LF delta turning positive to invalidate.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning
signals.text: EUR LF net short −72 (CFTC, week ending Apr 14); long%=50.0%. Positioning neutral but CFTC data shows a WoW delta −1,101 for the second consecutive week; AM not reducing. EUR/USD at 1.1751; price action stalling. Watch for LF delta turning positive or AM reducing to shift the read.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 3.75% vs Fed 3.75% — 0bp GBP carry neutral. GBP LF net long +28,426 (CFTC, week ending Apr 14); AM constructive — positioning aligned with the carry read. ATM IV 9.5% near HV, 25d RR flat — options neutral. GBP/USD at 1.3508 mid-range; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation.
